refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly include the operational period and the customer-defined target clarifies the scope and acceptance criteria for verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Monitor and log link availability metrics over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined threshold.
sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link uptime to support teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry data to monitor link status.

candidate_dps: DP1: Redundant network link architecture with failover capability.
DP2: Real-time link health monitoring and automatic recovery protocols.
DP3: Telemetry data aggregation and reporting system.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Redundant network link architecture with failover capability.

potential_sources: Link health monitoring tightly coupled with network link architecture.
Telemetry data interfaces with link monitoring.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is logically separable but requires coordination with DP2 and DP3; coupling is manageable with clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Risk of increased complexity leading to potential failure modes if not properly managed.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 as supporting DPs in subsequent decomposition levels to manage coupling.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for implementing network link redundancy, failover mechanisms, and link availability management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections managed for redundancy and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides link status and availability data to monitoring systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands contingent on link availability.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of link availability logic.
Vehicle module has limited direct control over network link redundancy.
Highest containment score; primary owner of teleoperation link availability.
Supports safety alerts but not directly responsible for link availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability logic within the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential single point of failure if Teleoperation module is compromised.
Inter-module dependencies with Safety Services and NGHE Solution require clear interface management.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define explicit interface contracts and fallback strategies with Safety Services and NGHE Solution to mitigate risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (redundant network link architecture) adequately satisfies the refined FR and addresses the high safety criticality.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as link availability logic is contained within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best IM boundary for the selected DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and link availability logs is feasible within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility for link availability to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Further decompose FRs to address link failure detection and recovery explicitly.
Clarify interface protocols between Teleoperation and Safety Services for safety alert integration.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of sub-FRs and supporting DPs is needed to fully address detection and recovery aspects and interface clarity.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link uptime to support teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry data to monitor link status.

revise.why: To explicitly capture detection and recovery functions and improve clarity for subsequent DP and IM assignments.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
open_risks: Complexity of failover mechanisms may introduce new failure modes.
Inter-module interface dependencies may cause integration challenges.
evidence_needed: Performance data on network link redundancy and failover effectiveness.
Verification results of telemetry accuracy and timeliness.
